title: Bulbophyllum ephippium
intro: Bulbophyllum ephippium, also known as the "ship-horn orchid," is a rare and unusual species of orchid native to the tropical forests of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bulbophyllum ephippium include alkaloids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which have been shown to possess antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a decoction or infusion, or dried and powdered for use in capsules or tinctures.
taxonomy: Bulbophyllum ephippium is classified within the family Orchidaceae, commonly known as the orchid family. It belongs to the genus Bulbophyllum, which comprises over 2,000 species of epiphytic orchids. The species Bulbophyllum ephippium is further classified into the section Ephippium. This classification is based on its unique characteristics and morphology. Further refinement of its classification may be possible through ongoing research and advancements in orchid taxonomy.

In some cultures, it is believed to have anti-inflammatory properties and is used to treat fever and respiratory issues.
